decision: Route users service construction through explicit dependency ports while keeping the legacy repository constructor as a compatibility wrapper.
reason: The scoped users package already had interface-segregated collaborators from PR581-027; this DIP pass removes the constructor's direct reliance on the fat repository abstraction without forcing broader call-site churn outside the scoped files.
impact: Tests can now build the service from explicit reader/writer/portfolio/credentials seams, while production callers can continue using NewService until they are migrated.

decision: Use explicit dependency bundle for users service
reason: Keeps high-level service logic and scoped tests bound to narrow ports while preserving existing production call sites through the legacy repository adapter.
impact: Minimizes constructor churn outside the scoped users package and improves DIP compliance in the targeted files.
